focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 6、INT. JACKIE'S VOLVO STATION WAGON - EARLY MORNING
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie drives her kids to school, discussing likes and dislikes with humor and affection, then bids them farewell with unique kisses before they head into the playground.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: child asserting individuality in a structured environment
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle voices specific dislikes and wears clothing that visually sets her apart from her peers at school.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Annabelle is observed as distinct from her peers, both in expression and appearance, by Jackie
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension between fitting in and expressing individuality
evidence_quotes: I don't care.
I hate overly ripe bananas -- they make me want to throw up.
I hate the crayon Burnt Sienna and people who spit when they walk.
The only orangey red dot in a sea of purple.

relation_updates: affirming bond with Jackie
status_updates: arrives at school in distinct clothing
auxiliary.persona_anchor: The only orangey red dot in a sea of purple

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Annabelle and Ben grow increasingly impatient as Rachel delays their lunch and costume pickup for a photo shoot; Annabelle feels ill and falls asleep while Rachel continues working.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: child experiencing neglect due to adult's work demands
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle grows increasingly hungry, bored, and physically unwell while waiting for Rachel to finish a photo shoot.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to eat, retrieve her costume, and prepare for the pageant
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Annabelle is exhausted, nauseous, and falls asleep on the bench
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: costume has not been picked up, pageant preparation remains delayed
evidence_quotes: A glum Annabelle and Ben sit on a bench watching Rachel at her photo shoot, George between them.
ANNABELLE But we're hungry — and I have to pick up my costume for the pageant! It starts at seven!
Annabelle and Ben roll their eyes.
Annabelle is asleep on the bench.

relation_updates: Annabelle's frustration with Rachel escalates
status_updates: Annabelle is physically exhausted and nauseous
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I feel like I'm gonna throw up

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie and Annabelle discuss Rachel's shower screams, leading to a candid, bittersweet conversation about sex, intimacy, and their unique bond, ending with a tender moment between mother and daughter.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: intimate mother-daughter dialogue
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle openly discusses sex and intimacy with her mother, responding with confidence and emotional clarity when prompted.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: explore adult topics with trusted parent
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: feels comfortable and affirmed in discussing intimate subjects with Jackie
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jackie's hidden emotional burden about 'waiting on news'
evidence_quotes: ANNABELLE Sex. Of course.
ANNABELLE During sex.
ANNABELLE I live. In the same country.
ANNABELLE I like talking about it. At least, to you.

relation_updates: deepens emotional intimacy with Jackie
establishes Annabelle as a confidante on sensitive subjects
status_updates: demonstrates increased emotional articulacy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I like talking about it—at least to you

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Annabelle accuses Jackie of lying about her cancer, refuses to believe her, and lashes out at her family before running out; Ben and Luke react with shock and concern.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional outburst and trust rupture
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle accuses Jackie of lying about her cancer diagnosis and rejects her, declaring she can never believe her again.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to protect herself from further emotional betrayal
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: distraught and defiant
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Jackie is telling the truth and whether the family can reconnect
evidence_quotes: You lied to us when you never told us!
If you lied then, maybe you're lying now. I can never belive you again!
She's dying and Rache is your mother now!
You are so STUPID!

relation_updates: estranged from Jackie
angry at Luke
dismissive of Ben
status_updates: in emotional crisis
actively disengaging from family
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I can't trust her—she lied to us

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 79、EXT. CAR POOL LINE, ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel tells Annabelle about an eighth-grade boy who finds her amusing, promises a handsome boy will appear with an expensive bike Monday, and shifts focus to junk food, seeking comfort after a bad day.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: strategic retaliation planning after failed nonviolence
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle abandons her mother's advice of non-confrontation after her attempt to 'use her words' results in mockery, and instead accepts Rachel's plan for public verbal retaliation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: restore social standing through public humiliation of the boy who mocked her
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: prepares to confront a peer using scripted insults focused on physical appearance
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: conflict between mother's pacifist values and Rachel's aggressive retaliation
evidence_quotes: ANNABELLE I did what she said. I used my words, I told him what I felt, and they...
RACHEL ...laughed, yeh. They laughed real hard.
ANNABELLE And Mom says anger never, never solves anything. It makes everything worse.

relation_updates: aligns with Rachel's confrontational approach
rejects mother's conflict-avoidance strategy
status_updates: shifts from victim to empowered aggressor
adopts new plan for public confrontation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: You're gonna use my words

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A father reassures his daughter of her mother's deep love, urging her to reciprocate when the moment comes, as they share a tender, hopeful connection.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional reckoning and truth disclosure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle learns that her father Luke felt conflicted about family life and resented sacrifices, leading to the separation, and that he withheld the truth to avoid blame, altering her understanding of her parents' roles in the breakup.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: processing parental conflict and emotional loyalty
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: quietly grieving but gaining emotional clarity about her parents' choices
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to reconcile love for both parents amid her father's admission of emotional ambivalence
evidence_quotes: LUKE ...that I didn't love her anymore...
LUKE She said she could never feel that way, not for one single second.
ANNABELLE Sure. You wanted me to blame her. Instead of you.

relation_updates: revised perception of Luke
renewed appreciation for her mother's commitment
status_updates: emotional maturity in handling complex feelings
auxiliary.persona_anchor: He wanted me to blame Mom... but she never stopped believing in us

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 101、INT. JACKIE'S VOLVO STATION WAGON - EARLY MORNING
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie drives her children to school, discussing likes and dislikes with them, sharing affectionate goodbyes, and watching them head into the playground while noting Annabelle's distinctiveness.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: establishing Annabelle's distinct identity and emotional expression
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle clearly states her dislikes, including the color Burnt Sienna and people who spit while walking, distinguishing herself from others in the moment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: resisting imposed norms, especially around color and behavior
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally expressive but defensive
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: conflict with Ben, discomfort with perceived judgment
evidence_quotes: ANNABELLE I hate the crayon Burnt Sienna and people who spit when they walk.
ANNABELLE I NEVER do that, you ALWAYS lie! And SLUGS him.
The only orangey red dot in a sea of purple.

relation_updates: tense interaction with Ben after being accused of thumb-sucking
affectionate but corrective dynamic with Jackie
status_updates: distinctive presence noted visually (only orangey-red dot in a sea of purple)
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I hate the crayon Burnt Sienna and people who spit when they walk

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Annabelle, portraying corn, is startled by Rachel's camera flashes, becomes disoriented and terrified, then runs offstage in tears; Jackie and Ben leave their seats as Luke looks on.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: child performer under pressure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle becomes disoriented and overwhelmed on stage after being startled by Rachel's camera flashes, leading her to burst into tears and run offstage.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To perform successfully in the pageant
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Devastated and fleeing the stage in tears
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: No resolution with Rachel; the incident remains unaddressed
evidence_quotes: Annabelle is the CORN.
Her camera FLASHES three times, quickly.
After each flash, we see the picture for a split second, Annabelle. A TERRIFIED ear of corn. A LOST ear of corn.
ANNABELLE (blinks, disoriented) Um...I'm...I'm...Oh...

relation_updates: Final rupture with Rachel's presence as disruptive
status_updates: Public humiliation
Emotional collapse
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I was the corn — and then I just couldn't anymore

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie and Annabelle share a candid, intimate conversation about sex, revealing a close, trusting mother-daughter bond through humor and vulnerability.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: intimate confidante
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle engages in a direct, thoughtful conversation with her mother about sex, showing openness and emotional awareness.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to understand and share openly about sexuality
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: a moment of mutual vulnerability and connection during a routine grooming activity
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jackie's emotional availability amid her own personal crisis
evidence_quotes: ANNABELLE I like talking about it. At least, to you.
ANNABELLE Why does Rachel scream? Does she mean what Jackie thinks she means?
ANNABELLE Because it feels really incredibly good.
Her mother laughs. Encouraged...

relation_updates: deepens trust with Jackie
status_updates: demonstrates emotional maturity beyond her years
positions herself as Jackie's emotional peer in private moments
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I can talk to Mom about anything, even the things that scare her

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Annabelle is gifted a video editor, sparking emotional gratitude and a heartfelt hug; Rachel watches, conflicted, as Jackie earns Annabelle's admiration, highlighting the trio's complex bond.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: receiving recognition and affirmation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle receives a video editor from Jackie, reacts with emotional gratitude, and affirms her bond with her, contrasting her interaction with Rachel.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: validate connection with Jackie
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: elated and emotionally bonded to Jackie
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension between loyalty to Jackie and relationship with Rachel
evidence_quotes: Annabelle insists it’s even better than the one Rachel had shown her.

relation_updates: strengthened bond with Jackie
public comparison with Rachel
status_updates: emotionally moved
validated in her passion
auxiliary.persona_anchor: My mom got me the editor — she’s the greatest

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie and Annabelle pause on a hill under the moon; Jackie tearfully vows to remember the moment, teaching Annabelle the 'never, never' loophole, and they share a tender promise of remembrance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional climax of mother-daughter connection
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle shares a quiet, intimate moment with her mother after a horseback ride, during which Jackie teaches her a repeated phrase as a way to affirm memory and commitment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to hold onto this moment forever
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: calm and comforted, feeling safe in her mother's embrace
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the fleeting nature of such moments
evidence_quotes: Annabelle relaxes against her mother and giggles with delight.
Jackie soon joins her, and the sounds of their laughter break the silence of the night.
ANNABELLE Never say never.
ANNABELLE I'll remember. Always, always.

relation_updates: deepens emotional bond with mother through shared ritual
status_updates: experiences profound emotional safety
gains a symbolic tool for commitment ('always, always')
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I'll remember. Always, always

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 179、EXT. CAR POOL LINE, ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Annabelle runs to Rachel, ecstatic that her plan worked, and hugs her tightly while Rachel's eyes meet Jackie's, signaling concern despite the embrace.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional turning point for Annabelle
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Annabelle receives a kiss on the cheek from a boy at school and runs to Rachel to share the moment, showing excitement and a desire to be acknowledged.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to feel validated and celebrated for her social success
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: excited and emotionally overwhelmed, seeking comfort and recognition from Rachel
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Rachel and Jackie's concern about the implications of Annabelle's new social attention
evidence_quotes: the boy leans, kisses Annabelle sweetly on the cheek.
she can't help breaking into a RUN, straight... ...into RACHEL'S ARMS.
ANNABELLE (breathless) It workeditworkedit worked! Omi god, you can't believe the look on his face!!!
Her eyes are locked to Jackie's. Houston. We have a problem.

relation_updates: strengthened bond with Rachel through physical affection
new peer connection with the boy from prep school
status_updates: experiences first public romantic gesture
becomes a figure of peer attention
auxiliary.persona_anchor: the day I ran to Mom and everything felt possible

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: The timeline traces 12 key developments across the character's screenplay trajectory.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie and Luke plan to contact Rachel, but recall a failed conference call; the meeting ends as a fight echoes down the school hallway.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: co-parent in conflict with ex-husband and his partner
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie challenges Luke and the school counselor about Annabelle's fantasy, emphasizing concerns about Rachel's role in the children's lives.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: protect children from instability caused by Luke's new relationship
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jackie asserts herself as the attentive parent amid growing conflict with Luke over household changes and Rachel's involvement.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing disagreement over parenting decisions and the impact of Rachel's presence on the children
evidence_quotes: JACKIE: Mrs. Franklin we're not -- LUKE: Planning on getting -- JACKIE: Remarried. There is no move.
JACKIE: Excuse him. He never learned how to turn the darn thing off. And reaches. Does it for him.
JACKIE (suddenly fierce): This man would walk thru fire for Annabelle, gladly, any day of the week.
MRS. FRANKLIN: I'm wondering if Annabelle is responding to the underlying hostility that exists between Rachel and Mrs. Harrison...

relation_updates: escalated conflict with Luke over Rachel moving in
reinforced adversarial stance toward Rachel Kelly
status_updates: actively advocating for children in institutional setting
auxiliary.persona_anchor: when I defended Annabelle's feelings in that counselor's office, even though Luke wasn't listening

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie and Annabelle discuss Rachel's shower screams, leading to a candid, bittersweet conversation about sex, intimacy, and their unique bond, ending with a tender moment between mother and daughter.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: mother engaging in intimate emotional conversation with daughter
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie has a candid conversation with Annabelle about sex and intimacy, sharing a moment of emotional openness and connection while brushing her hair.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jackie reveals a vulnerable and reflective emotional state during a private moment with her daughter.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jackie mentions she is waiting on some news, but does not specify what it is.
evidence_quotes: Jackie brushing out Annabelle's hair. Jackie's eyes are distant.
JACKIE Oh, I'm waiting on some news, that's all.
ANNABELLE Because it feels really incredibly good.
JACKIE (a murmur) Same here, huh?

relation_updates: emotional intimacy deepened with Annabelle
auxiliary.persona_anchor: brushing her hair and talking about what feels good

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie and Luke argue over his upcoming marriage to Rachel; Luke wants to tell their children together, but Jackie refuses, resentful of his past behavior and unwilling to co-parent on his terms.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: ex-wife confronting partner's remarriage
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Luke announces his engagement to Rachel and asks Jackie to help tell the children; Jackie refuses, rejecting his request due to lingering resentment and perceived inconsistency in his commitment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to resist participating in a performance of unity she does not feel
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally guarded and defiant toward Luke
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to co-parent amid ongoing tension
evidence_quotes: LUKE I'm gonna marry Rachel.
JACKIE You can't be an 'us' just when you want to. You can't play that card when it's convenient.
JACKIE No. You think this is going to help the kids then you do it. You're on your own.

relation_updates: rejects co-parenting request from Luke
expresses unresolved resentment toward Luke
status_updates: Luke engaged to Rachel
auxiliary.persona_anchor: You can't be an 'us' just when you want to

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie and Luke share a warm, nostalgic exchange in Ben's hospital room, discussing Rachel, family dynamics, and weekend plans, while Ben awaits Jackie with innocent curiosity.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: mother and caregiver observing family moment
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie observes Ben interacting affectionately with Rachel while sharing a reflective moment with Luke, acknowledging Rachel's presence in Ben's life with cautious acceptance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintaining connection with Ben while navigating new relationships
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: accepting of change but guarded
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension around Annabel and shifting family roles
evidence_quotes: Jackie stands there. Her eyes are difficult to read. But she sure is watching.
LUKE (softly) Nice, huh?
JACKIE It is. It's about time.
LUKE You want us to take him tonight? Give you some private time with Annab....

relation_updates: reaffirms bond with Ben
reconnects briefly with Luke in a familiar, affectionate way
acknowledges Rachel's growing role in Ben's life
status_updates: emotionally present but reserved
maintains caregiving responsibility
auxiliary.persona_anchor: watching Ben sing with someone else, feeling both warmth and distance

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben, stranded in a tree, believes he must reach heaven to correct a mistake; Jessica the firewoman rescues him, reuniting him with Jackie as onlookers cheer, though Jackie remains fearful.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: responding to Ben's dangerous climb as a life-threatening emergency
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie physically and emotionally reacts to Ben's peril with intense fear, gripping the firefighter tightly and shouting to Ben, revealing her vulnerability beneath her usual strength.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: safely retrieve Ben from the tree and protect him
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jackie holds Ben tightly after rescue, visibly relieved but internally aware that the danger has not fully passed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ben's belief that Jackie is dying and must be replaced by Grammy or Grandpa
evidence_quotes: she looks up screaming -- JACKIE BEEEENNNNNN!
JACKIE Don't move!
Jackie holds tightly onto JESSICA, a diminutive five-foot-tall Firewoman, as they soar skyward towards Ben.
Jackie GRASPS Ben, pulling him FIERCELY to her arms in a death grip.

relation_updates: reaffirmed physical and emotional bond with Ben
status_updates: exposed as fearful despite outward strength
auxiliary.persona_anchor: God doesn't like visitors—but if He takes me, He better take Grammy or Grandpa first.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel confronts Jackie about sacrifices in motherhood; Jackie hints at unresolved truths her partner dismisses as history, sparking Rachel's urgent curiosity.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: mother sharing birth experiences and hinting at marital tensions
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie recounts the contrasting births of her children and responds to Rachel's question about unresolved sacrifices in her marriage, acknowledging her partner dismissed it as 'history' but implying he got part of it wrong.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to convey her children's inherent natures and her own unacknowledged sacrifices
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: acknowledges that her partner minimized significant personal history, leaving parts of it unspoken and unresolved
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: what specific sacrifice or truth Jackie's partner dismissed as 'history' that Rachel now seeks to uncover
evidence_quotes: Ben was born in two hours, went right to my breast and camped there for three days.
Took 28 hours. She just wasn't sure about entering this world.
Don't let anybody rush her.
He won't discuss it. Just calls it history.

relation_updates: invites Rachel into deeper emotional confidence
status_updates: confirms biological kinship with Ben and daughter
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I told Rachel about Ben’s birth, about Annabelle taking her time—I won’t let anyone rush her

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 101、INT. JACKIE'S VOLVO STATION WAGON - EARLY MORNING
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie drives her children to school, discussing likes and dislikes with them, sharing affectionate goodbyes, and watching them head into the playground while noting Annabelle's distinctiveness.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jackie acts as a hands-on, rule-guided parent during a morning school drop-off
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie manages her children's behavior, engages in a structured exchange about dislikes, and shares affectionate goodbye rituals during the drive to school.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jackie remains actively engaged in her children's emotional and physical care, reinforcing family routines as they part for the school day
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: children's exposure to parental conflict through references to Rachel wearing their father's underpants
evidence_quotes: JACKIE Never say 'never' -- it's not fair to say 'always' -- and no name calling. Use your words.
Eskimo Kiss. (they rub noses) Russian Orthodox Wedding Kiss. They bump foreheads and elbows.
She collects them and hands Ben and Annabelle two brightly colored lunch bags.
JACKIE I noticed a whopping pile of laundry sitting on the washer -- perhaps Rachel's underpants are in there

relation_updates: strengthens bond with Annabelle and Ben
models emotional expression through shared 'hates' conversation
status_updates: Jackie enforces family rules
demonstrates attentive caregiving
auxiliary.persona_anchor: the nose-to-nose and forehead-to-forehead goodbye ritual

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie confronts Luke and Rachel over changed plans, blaming a missed message; she warns Rachel before hanging up, leaving Rachel shaken.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: mother enforcing boundaries via phone
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie confronts Luke and Rachel about changed plans, questions whether Rachel forgot the message, mocks the excuse, and issues a direct warning to Rachel before hanging up.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: defend her decision to change plans and protect her time with the children
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jackie asserts control over the children's schedule and communicates distrust of Rachel, leaving Rachel shaken.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Rachel's role in the family remains uncertain and tense
evidence_quotes: JACKIE (innocent) ...until the plan changed, when Jessie's mom gave us these tickets.
What, Rachel. 'forgot' I told her?
Don't tell me. Another machine ate another message?
Think twice. Before you ever pull that again.

relation_updates: issues direct threat to Rachel
deepens conflict with Luke
status_updates: asserts control over children's schedule
positions herself as the responsible parent
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Think twice. Before you ever pull that again.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie leaves Rachel in charge of the kids with detailed care instructions, escalating tension over responsibility and trust, while both exchange sarcastic remarks amid a strained standoff.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: mother asserting control in a conflict
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie confronts Rachel about taking the blame for her, then gives her detailed instructions for caring for her children despite mutual distrust and hostility.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure reliable care for her children despite distrust
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: temporarily delegates childcare to Rachel under strict conditions, maintaining emotional distance
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Rachel will follow through and whether minimal cooperation can be sustained
evidence_quotes: JACKIE I'm sorry. Look, I'm not real comfortable being here, but...
JACKIE I overhead what you told Annabelle. The lie.
All I ask is that they're alive when I get there.

relation_updates: escalated tension with Rachel
conditional delegation of responsibility
status_updates: formally delegates temporary care of children
asserts high standards for their treatment
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I didn’t want to ask her, but I had to—I left everything she’d need, just in case

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie rescues Ben from a frozen food bin, hugging him desperately; Ben says he knew she'd find him because he's her priority, and she affirms it.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: desperate mother
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie rescues Ben from a frozen food bin and affirms he is her priority after he states he knew she would find him because he matters most to her.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reaffirm commitment to Ben
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jackie physically retrieves Ben and emotionally reaffirms her devotion to him in the moment
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: balancing her illness with parenting
evidence_quotes: There, lying on his back atop dwindling stores of Breyer's ice cream...is Ben. Hands and beaming face PRESSED to the underside of the glass.
JACKIE YYYAAAAAAAHHH! ...and FLINGS OPEN Snow White's glass coffin.
BEN You found me cause I'm your priority , huh?
JACKIE You got that right.

relation_updates: reestablishes emotional bond with Ben
status_updates: overwhelmed but present
emotionally raw
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I crushed him in my arms and meant every word

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Annabelle questions if her mom can reconnect with her younger self; Jackie remains confident. A breathless Tucker suddenly appears, asking how tall her tree is and how bad a fall from it would hurt.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: providing emotional guidance during a private moment
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Advises Annabelle to ignore a boy who teases her at school, framing it as a strategy to deny him attention and stop the behavior.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Help Annabelle regain emotional control
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Annabelle appears reassured and adopts a determined expression after her mother's advice
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether ignoring the boy will effectively stop the teasing
evidence_quotes: ANNABELLE This'll work, huh? JACKIE Oh, yeh. Annabelle takes heart from her mom's confidence. Nods, with her trademark determination.

relation_updates: reinforces supportive mother-daughter bond
status_updates: establishes herself as a source of strength
auxiliary.persona_anchor: You're too much of a woman to bother with little boys

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel asserts her individuality, rejecting the pressure to emulate Jackie as a mother, and resolves to confront their strained relationship on her own terms.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: confronting Rachel about undermining her parenting
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie confronts Rachel for encouraging Annabelle to lie and use offensive language, asserting her belief in moral development over winning.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reassert control over her child's upbringing
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally charged and defensive
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to maintain influence over Annabelle's values
evidence_quotes: JACKIE (low, calm) Now we're going to have a fight, you and I.
JACKIE You put filth in my child's mouth.
JACKIE You had her lie about that... that fancy-boy model !
JACKIE See, in that crisis, I saw an opportunity. For some real growth.

relation_updates: escalates conflict with Rachel
status_updates: positioned as authoritative but challenged mother
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I saw an opportunity for real growth, not just winning

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel Kelly wakes to a blaring alarm, rushes out half-dressed, returns for a robe, and runs down the hall with a wall hook stuck in her back.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: introducing chaotic domestic routine
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel wakes abruptly, rushes out of her bedroom in partial clothing, and runs down the hall with a wall hook stuck in her back without noticing.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: get out the door quickly
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: rushed and physically disheveled, with a hook unknowingly embedded in her back
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of control over routine
evidence_quotes: Rachel SITS BOLTS UPRIGHT.
LEAPS out of the room.
From the back we see that she's dressed only in a pair of men's boxers.
She FLINGS her robe on as she RUNS down the hall, wall hook STICKING OUT of her back.

status_updates: physically disheveled
rushed and distracted
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I ran out the door with a hook stuck in my back and didn't even notice

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel and Jackie clash over Annabelle walking in on Rachel and Luke; Jackie criticizes Rachel's avoidance and parenting, leading to a heated argument and Rachel being told to leave.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: conflict escalation with Jackie
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel is confronted by Jackie after Annabelle walks in on her and Luke in the shower, leading to a heated argument in which Jackie rejects Rachel’s presence and tells her to leave the house.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Avoid conflict and maintain access to the children
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rachel is expelled from the household and no longer accepted as a maternal presence by Jackie
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Annabelle's emotional reaction to seeing Rachel and Luke together is unaddressed
evidence_quotes: Luke was in the shower this morning and Annabelle sort of walked in without knocking.
I was in there in him.
This isn't going to work out.
Get out!

relation_updates: Jackie views Rachel as unfit and self-centered
Rachel views Jackie as imperious and hostile
status_updates: Relationship with Jackie becomes irreparably hostile
Parenting role challenged by Jackie
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I just wanted to connect with Annabelle through photography

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel reluctantly agrees to take the children to the park after Jackie lists detailed care instructions, highlighting tension and mutual distrust amid childcare responsibilities.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: reluctant childcare provider in a tense negotiation with Jackie
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel agrees to take Jackie's children to the park after a confrontational exchange involving detailed care instructions and mutual distrust.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Rachel commits to temporary childcare despite resistance and sarcasm
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rachel takes responsibility for the children, accepting supplies and a schedule from Jackie
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: mutual distrust and lack of clear boundaries in their co-caregiving arrangement
evidence_quotes: RACHEL (simply) I didn't do it for you. Believe me.
RACHEL (dry) Thank you. And scoops up the parenting paraphernalia.

relation_updates: increased tension with Jackie
conditional cooperation established
status_updates: assumes temporary caregiver role
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I didn't do it for you. I did it for her.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 59、EXT. A PERFECTLY MANICURED TWO STORY HOME - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel manages Ben's outburst and a demanding work call, asserting control amid judgmental mothers, then calmly addresses a crisis, reassuring Ben about his struggles.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Rachel navigates conflicting family and work demands during a public incident
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel handles Ben's disruptive behavior at a birthday party while managing an urgent work call, ultimately ending the call to address Annab's academic crisis when she calls in distress.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Ensure Annab succeeds despite difficulties
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rachel disengages from a high-pressure work call to focus on Annab's emotional and academic needs
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Conflict between Rachel's professional obligations and family responsibilities remains unresolved
evidence_quotes: Goddamn it Ben! Let go of it. NOW!
She SLAMS the phone SHUT.
Get over it, Dunc... (stops) ...whoa, whoa, Annab...
Flunking science is not happening. On my watch.

relation_updates: Rachel establishes firm but caring control over Ben
Rachel reassures Annab with personal responsibility
status_updates: Rachel is seen as disruptive by suburban mothers
Rachel prioritizes family over professional pressure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Flunking science is not happening on my watch

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel defies Duncan, refuses to continue filming, and quits abruptly, prioritizing her children over her career despite his warnings.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: professional photographer and mother
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel stops working mid-shoot, hands off her camera, and leaves the set to pick up her child, defying her employer's demands.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: prioritize her child's needs over completing a job
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rachel has quit her photography job and is no longer part of the production team.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: potential long-term impact on her career from abruptly quitting
evidence_quotes: At last, she lowers her camera.
Checks her watch.
And HANDS HIM the camera.
RACHEL I. Quit.

relation_updates: defies Duncan's authority
aligns with Jackie's protective maternal identity
status_updates: exits professional photography role
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I. Quit.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Luke and Rachel grapple with broken promises and parental guilt; she confronts him about their daughter and the family's collapse, then leaves to drink alone, deepening their emotional rift.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional rupture and separation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel confronts Luke about the impact of his actions on their daughter and the breakdown of their family, then walks away to drink alone, signaling a definitive emotional separation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to assert control by removing herself from a failing dynamic
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rachel physically and emotionally withdraws from Luke, choosing solitude after the confrontation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Rachel will return to the relationship or continue toward emotional independence
evidence_quotes: Maybe Annabelle deserves to find out who really broke up her family.
You got one less for dinner.
I'm gonna get me a drink.
And I'm gonna drink it alone.

relation_updates: Rachel severs emotional connection with Luke
She implies Annabelle deserves the truth about her family's collapse
status_updates: Rachel is now physically and emotionally isolated
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I'm gonna drink it alone

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel, overwhelmed with emotion, reads Jackie's promised five-word message—'MY BABIES. ARE SO LUCKY'—at the wedding, fulfilling a heartfelt vow before the gathering.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: public fulfillment of a private vow
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel reads Jackie's final message at her wedding, honoring a promise made to her and acknowledging Jackie's enduring presence through her children.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: fulfill a sacred promise to Jackie
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rachel is visibly moved to tears after reading the message, publicly expressing grief and connection
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to carry Jackie forward without breaking
evidence_quotes: RACHEL So Jackie and I were sitting around. On New Year's Eve. And she said, 'You're not gonna talk at my funeral, are you?'
RACHEL ...my two sidekicks there always bring her along. Wherever they go.
RACHEL It's...no big thing, really... it's just...five words, it's...MY BABIES. ARE SO LUCKY.

relation_updates: reaffirms Jackie's lasting influence in her life
connects Jackie to her children as ongoing legacy
status_updates: publicly moved, tears break emotional restraint
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I read your five words and finally let myself cry

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel reveals Annabelle walked in on her and Luke showering; Jackie criticizes Rachel's avoidance, sparking a heated argument about parenting, boundaries, and responsibility, ending with Jackie demanding Rachel leave.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: central conflict with Jackie
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel reveals she was showering with Luke when Annabelle walked in, dismisses the need to discuss the incident with her, and responds to Jackie's criticism with defensiveness and hostility, leading to a breakdown in communication and her expulsion from the house.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to connect with Annabelle through shared interests and gain Jackie's approval
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: expelled from the house and rejected by Jackie as a co-parenting figure
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Annabelle's emotional reaction to walking in on Rachel and Luke is not addressed
evidence_quotes: Luke was in the shower this morning and Annabelle sort of walked in without knocking.
I was in there in him.
No. I thought it might be uncomfortable for her --
You're damn right. I'm gonna sick of your imperious bullshit.

relation_updates: relationship with Jackie deteriorates into open hostility
parenting approach challenged by Jackie
status_updates: accused of emotional avoidance
excluded from Jackie's household
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I was just trying to connect with her through photography

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel confronts a personal crisis while managing a high-pressure work environment surrounded by evidence of flying canaries, as Cooper reminds her of an impending client meeting.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: personal and professional crisis convergence
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel learns she failed to pick up Annabelle, leading to emotional distress while managing urgent work demands.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Balancing professional responsibilities with personal caregiving duties
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rachel is emotionally deflated and acknowledges her failure to fulfill a caregiving responsibility due to work pressures.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Rachel will manage the conflict between her work obligations and her role as a caregiver.
evidence_quotes: RACHEL (into phone) ...no, no, that is not possible. You must have the wrong little gir...
All the air comes out. She looks around, sadly, at all the work surrounding her.

relation_updates: Rachel expresses trust in Annabelle's mother's reliability
status_updates: Rachel is overwhelmed and emotionally deflated
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I let a child down because I was buried in proofs

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie and Rachel rush to Ben after his fall; Rachel arrives first but lets Jackie comfort him. Ben, injured, worries about attending Tucker's party.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: confidante and emotional anchor for Jackie during a moment of personal uncertainty
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie reveals to Rachel her consideration of returning to part-time work at Random House, indicating a shift in her priorities and emotional state.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: considering returning to work at Random House part-time
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jackie expresses hesitation about her decision and asks Rachel to keep it secret, signaling ambivalence about moving forward
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jackie avoids direct eye contact and resists committing fully to the decision or informing her family
evidence_quotes: Thinking. Deciding if this is the time to say...
...thinking of going back to Random House. On a part-time basis.
Well, I could do most of it from home. But, while I'm working it out with the head editor, I'd need to... Sighs. Boy, this is tough.
Let's...hold up for a bit. It may not happen. Our secret, okay?

relation_updates: Jackie and Rachel share a moment of mutual vulnerability
status_updates: Jackie plans potential work trips to the city
auxiliary.persona_anchor: telling Rachel about going back to work while avoiding eye contact

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie urges perseverance and patience in enduring pain, while Rachel hides her disappointment and softly acknowledges Jackie's stance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional confrontation with Jackie's coping strategy
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel hides her disappointment when Jackie advocates enduring pain without intervention, revealing her internal conflict about this approach.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: understanding Jackie's stance while suppressing disagreement
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally conflicted but compliant
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: doubt about the validity of enduring pain without resistance
evidence_quotes: Oh. Rachel tries to hide her disappointment.
She has to stick with it. Have the patience, the guts, to ignore the pain. You disagree?
Half a beat. Rachel shakes her head, nope.
RACHEL (softly) Hey. You oughta know.

relation_updates: tension beneath surface loyalty to Jackie
status_updates: witnesses Jackie using marijuana for pain
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I tried to hide how disappointed I was when she said keep going, just endure it

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rachel confronts Luke about broken promises and their daughter Annabelle, then walks away to drink alone, deepening the emotional rift between them.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: climactic emotional break with Luke
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rachel confronts Luke about broken promises and their impact on Annabelle, then ends the conversation and walks away to drink alone.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to hold Luke accountable for the impact on their children
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally disengaged from Luke and choosing isolation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to protect Annabelle from the fallout of parental conflict
evidence_quotes: Maybe Annabelle deserves to find out who really broke up her family. While her mom is still around.
He puts his hands on her, gently. But she flinches. So the hands come away.
LUKE Where you goin'?

relation_updates: rejects physical contact from Luke
ends conversation definitively
status_updates: chooses solitude over connection
publicly disengages from Luke
